title: Guest Lecture On “workshop On Ai – Driven ” Campus To Corporate Cracking Internships & Placements
content: Event Date: 03-12-2025 Event Brief Description The School of Finance and Commerce (SFC), Galgotias University, organized a workshop titled “AI-Driven: Campus to Corporate – Cracking Internships & Placements” on 3rd December 2025 at the New Auditorium, C-Block. The session was conducted by Mr. Prasun Choudhary, who highlighted the growing role of Artificial Intelligence in modern recruitment processes, including resume screening, skill assessment, and virtual interviews. He provided practical insights on enhancing internship and placement readiness using AI-based tools for resume building, mock interviews, and communication improvement. The workshop emphasized adapting to evolving hiring trends and aligned with the university’s commitment to quality education and holistic student development. Event Detailed Description The School of Finance and Commerce (SFC), Galgotias University, successfully organized an insightful workshop titled “AI-Driven: Campus to Corporate – Cracking Internships & Placements” on 3rd December 2025 at the New Auditorium, C-Block. Held from 12 A.M. to 4 P.M., the session witnessed enthusiastic participation from students keen to understand the role of Artificial Intelligence in shaping modern corporate careers. The workshop was conducted by Mr. Prasun Choudhary, a seasoned professional with expertise in contemporary hiring practices and emerging technologies. He elaborated on the increasing integration of AI in recruitment processes, including resume screening, behavioral assessment, skill mapping, and virtual interviews. Through practical examples and live demonstrations, he guided students on adapting to AI-driven evaluation systems to enhance their competitiveness. Focusing on internship and placement readiness, Mr. Choudhary introduced AI-powered tools that support resume building, mock interview practice, communication enhancement, and personal skill assessment. He emphasized the significance of cultivating a strong digital presence and developing essential workplace competencies. The session encouraged students to leverage AI as a strategic ally for professional growth and productivity. The workshop also reflected the university’s commitment to holistic development and the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs), particularly Quality Education, Gender Equality, Decent Work and Economic Growth, and Partnerships for the Goals. Overall, the event was highly appreciated for its relevance, clarity, and practical insights. Department Name : School of Finance and Commerce The workshop enhanced students’ understanding of AI-driven recruitment processes and modern hiring trends. Participants learned to optimize resumes, prepare for virtual interviews, and use AI tools for skill development and placement readiness. Overall, the session strengthened their confidence and bridged the gap between campus learning and corporate expectations.
